item.title: 10 Things I Love About Trail Running RIGHT NOW
item.itunes:title: 10 Things I Love About Trail Running RIGHT NOW
item.description:
It's easy to love trail running this time of year because in the northern hemisphere we're leaving winter for spring and all the good feelings of hope that come with it.

I want to add to that good will and sentiment by naming the 10 things the I love about trail running right now not the least of which is ACTUAL trail and ultra running.

  1. 01:23 ACTUAL trail running
  2. 10:48 Ultratrail Race Season
  3. 13:39 Running to "Overcome"
  4. 16:23 Ciele Athletics
  5. 18:39 Trail Shoe Design
  6. 24:05 Scrappy Trail Running Brands
  7. 30:37 Meme Culture
  8. 35:08 Tommie Runz
  9. 36:21 Taylor Bodin (dirt division) + Josh Bouton (meta endurance)
  10. 38:27 Inky Steve, Stan Van Kemmel, Bryce Carlson, Sam Lohse

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: How Ciele Athletics Survived Since Circa 2014
item.itunes:title: How Ciele Athletics Survived Since Circa 2014
item.description:
Jeremy Bresnen founded Ciele Athletics Inc./Ciele Athlétique Inc. with Mike Giles in 2014 in the now-great running city of Montreal. I started running around 2012 and clearly remember seeing Ciele on the shelf at Salt Lake Running Company circa 2015.

I bought a hat almost instinctively and no doubt irresistibly.

I wasn't sure I wanted to even run in it yet becuase I loved it so much and didn't want to ruin it from the 100 degree July days. Eventually I bought a second Ciele hat - one for running and one for around town.

It was the first brand I wore while not running to signal to everyone that I was a runner. Ciele transcended any single type of running and permeated anywhere runners cared about performance and style - road, trail, and track from 5k to ultra distances.

They emerged with a cohort of brands still going today like Tracksmith, Oiselle, Janji, and SATISFY. This co-hort of early 2010s brands were all seeing something similar - people were not simply running - they were runners.

Jeremy Bresnen tells the story of Ciele while on a long walk with me from founding to today with thoughts on the future. From the days of hats-only to today where they create all sorts of running apparel and different styles of hats all built around performance first then style.

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. High Tones Substack by Sam Lohse
  4. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  5. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Camille Herron | World Record Collector, Endurance + Ultra Running Legend
item.itunes:title: Camille Herron | World Record Collector, Endurance + Ultra Running Legend
item.description:
Camille Herron is an endurance and ultra running [world] Record Collector on the road, trail, and track.

She's committed to pushing back the boundaries and guardrails put on women's sports with a list of accomplishments that should make anyone listen.

Enjoy this conversation about Camille's great accomplishments with emphasis on Lululemon's 6 Day event, Spartathalon, Comrades, IAU Championships, and more.

Some takeaways:

  1. Camille switched from road to trail + felt at home
  2. She sets mini goals during races to make it feel attainable
  3. Pain perception [or lack thereof] is her superpower
  4. She caps long runs at two hours during training
  5. Training should be tailored to individual needs
  6. [especially for neurodivergent athletes]
  7. The camaraderie in ultra running welcomes everyone.
  8. Spartathlon and Comrades connect runners to a rich history + heritage

She believes she has many record breaking years ahead of her - what are her goals?

-

Josh brings back the classic Borderlands [DFL Before DNF - iykyk] question for Camille - "how do you keep going when you want to quit?" No doubt in Camille's intense and rigorous endurance records she has wanted to quit but hasn't. So how does she keep going?

-

A few years ago she noticed that she could incredible race output from decreasing her long runs down to 2 hours max. She later found a French author and researcher who wrote a book as he chased the idea.

After adopting it, Camille has enjoyed less injuries and an overall increase in performance.

[Hosts note: Candidly, we set out to talk about the not-so-recent 'Wikipedia situtation' but as the conversation was going I decided to stay in the moment of celebrating an unbelievable career. I hope to have Camille on multiple times in the coming years where we can talk about this and so many other things important to her and ultra running.]

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Jesse Rich | Ultra Running Nutrition vs. Nourishment
item.itunes:title: Jesse Rich | Ultra Running Nutrition vs. Nourishment
item.description:
In this episode we reframe endurance nutrition to thinking about endurance nourishment.

In efforts under 24 hours, its reasonable to thinkg about it in simple terms of 'nutrition' but in these big multi-day ultra running efforts where you're sleep-deprived and putting enormous pressure on your system you have to think about nourishment. At some point you have to go beyond considering electorlytes and begin considering all the nutrients - fat, protien, etc.

  1. How many calories do we burn in a 100 miler?
  2. How many calories are our body burning in days following without effort?
  3. How efficiently are we absorbing calories after one day of consecutive endurance effort?
  4. Is there a connection between sleep and absorbing nutrition?
  5. When you are at a nutritional deficiency in a race, can you catch back up?
  6. Post-multi day run recover requires immense calories.
  7. Caffeine is useful only if strategically timed.

This episode offer good advice for anyone running The Speed Project or any multi-day endurance race or run.

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Anne Flower | Black Canyon Ultras + Balancing Life as a Physician
item.itunes:title: Anne Flower | Black Canyon Ultras + Balancing Life as a Physician
item.description:
There's no such thing as an overnight success but Anne Flower sure felt like it in the last year. She appeared virtually out of no where to snag the Leadville 100 course record from the great Anne Trason, set the 50 mile world record at Tunnel Hill, and get the overall Win at Javelina Jundred.

But its not true. She's been running hard for a long time but her first passion has been in the drivers seat - her career as a physician in Colorado Springs.

She's racing Black Canyon Ultras - 100k - to test herself against a super speedy field and she sat down to talk with Josh to talk shop about her career to this point and how she's thinking about BC specifically.

  1. Anne's insights on training
  2. Anne's insights on ultra running from a physician's perspective
  3. Her approach compared to David Roche's as the two NEW Leadville CR holders
  4. Her thoughts on Black Canyon's lack of prize money
  5. Her fondness for Moab trail racing
  6. Her perspective as an elite runner and sponsorships while remaining hyperfocused on her non-running career.

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Francesco Puppi | Black Canyon, Running the American West, + Prize Money
item.itunes:title: Francesco Puppi | Black Canyon, Running the American West, + Prize Money
item.description:
Francesco Puppi has been ultra trail running for a long time, but his 2025 wins at UTMB's CCC, The Canyons 100k, and Chianti 47k put him squarely on the radar of the average American fan.

Anytime Francesco Puppi is in a race, he's a contender and one that the other contenders have to account for. The Black Canyon course suits his marathon speed even with its techincality.

He wins big mountain races but is speedy when the course suits.

No matter how the race shakes out, Francesco Puppi will make it fun for fans simply by bringing his gifts to the race and course.

Presented by Kiprun.

Hosted by Josh Rosenthal, founder of Borderlands Trail Running.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Alex Zono | Black Canyon Ultras + "Running is My Best Friend"
item.itunes:title: Alex Zono | Black Canyon Ultras + "Running is My Best Friend"
item.description:
Alex Zono designs running gear for anyone who truly digs running. He puts his heart and soul into it and its not necessarily optimized for a pace, but a mindset and spirit.

But don't let that playful lightheartedness fool you, Alex Zono is going to run Black Canyon 50k with intent to finish on the leaderboard.

Go see his apparel line and you'll feel the juxtaposition of a brand truly in its element at Paris Fashion Week and a runner truly in his element at Black Canyon 50k.

In this episode, Alex sits down with Josh Rosenthal and Sam Lohse in Borderlands Studio at Paris Fashion Week to talk about his love American dirtbag ultra and trail running, design, and the world of Paris Fashion Week for runners.

"Running is My Best Friend" reference is from Alex Zono's collection.

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Paris Fashion Week is for Runners
item.itunes:title: Paris Fashion Week is for Runners
item.description:
Josh Rosenthal, Inky Steve, Sam Lohse, Mike Kratzer, and Simon Freeman (co-founder of Like the Wind) sat down in the Borderlands Podcast Studio at Paris Fashion Week to talk about the purpose of PFW and why its important for runners to pay attention to it.

You certianly don't have to, but if you're curious or care at all about what's happening with Running Apparel and Clothing, its a brilliant event. Its not for consumers though. It's a classic B2B event in random shops in the 3rd Arrondissement of Paris.

There is no central hub telling you what shop is where. You only know where to go if you know where to go. Some brands post on social while others want to be 'found' more organically.

It's all interesting. But also useful.

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Paul Mellor | Why Should Runners Pay Attention to the Trail Running Industry?
item.itunes:title: Paul Mellor | Why Should Runners Pay Attention to the Trail Running Industry?
item.description:
Paul Mellor joins Josh on the show — founder of underdog-focused creative agency Mellor&Smith, executive producer and 4x BAFTA nominee on Boiling Point, and currently working on a fresh documentary about ultrarunning legend Lazarus Lake.

We dive into why trail runners should care about the bigger outdoor industry, Nike's surprisingly humble return to trail running, what makes certain brands unforgettable (and why others fade into the background), and the power of standing out as an underdog.

Presented by Kiprun.

Paul Mellor is the founder of Mellor&Smith, a creative advertising agency that specializes in helping underdog outdoor brands — those not yet dominating their category — get noticed by the world. His clients include some of the biggest names in outdoor and adventure who are still fighting for pole position.

Beyond advertising, Paul is an executive producer and four-time BAFTA nominee for the acclaimed film Boiling Point (yes, that intense one-take kitchen drama). He's currently deep into producing a new documentary on Lazarus Lake (Laz), the mythical creator of the Barkley Marathons and Big's Backyard Ultra — offering a perspective on Laz you've never seen before.

  1. Why trail runners and the trail community should pay attention to the wider outdoor industry
  2. Nike's re-entry into the trail running space — why acg feels refreshingly humble
  3. The ingredients that make certain brands memorable in a crowded market
  4. Why some brands struggle to stand out and may never truly resonate
  5. The mindset and creative approaches that help underdogs punch above their weight and get noticed
  6. A sneak peek into Paul's work on the upcoming Lazarus Lake documentary and what makes this take unique.

Connect with Paul Mellor on LinkedIn

Resources & Links:

  1. Nike ACG
  2. Rapha
  3. Satisfy
  4. The Speed Project
  5. Kantar Research — Insights on brand investment effectiveness
  6. Paul Dyson & 2009 + 2020 Media Study — On brand allocation and effective marketing levers
  7. Warren Buffett on Allocation — Strategic resource distribution insights

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Tommy Lewis | Run Influencing is Over, Long Live Running
item.itunes:title: Tommy Lewis | Run Influencing is Over, Long Live Running
item.description:
Tommy Lewis and Josh Rosenthal talk about how they're a bit tired with the business of running (content and everything else) but they share a continued commitment and fascination with running itself.

Tommy is the final guest in our series celebrating those who bring light to the world through running. Others have included Laz Lake, Nils Arend, Scott Jurek, and Dean Karnazes.

He's a content creator and influencer buildling communities around running sustainabily with a sense of purpose and joy.

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Lazarus Lake [pt 3] | A Conversation on Laz's Front Porch
item.itunes:title: Lazarus Lake [pt 3] | A Conversation on Laz's Front Porch
item.description:
Lazarus Lake (Gary Cantrell) is the race director and creator of the Barley Marathons and Big's Backyard Ultra among others.

In this interview, Josh pays homage to him with three artifacts designed to spur on conversation and attempt to explain more of who Laz Lake actually is.

1. Cigarettes | Laz smokes Camel Yellows. We talk about his origins of smoking.

2. LIke the Wind magazine | Mechanical watch companies navigated the quartz crisis, Like the Wind navigated the digital crises. Laz, like LtW and Blancpain, they never got sucked in to the crises and stayed convicted and committed to their way of doing things - untouched by modernization.

3. Old Man and the Sea by Ernest Hemingway | Laz is like Santiago from the old man and the sea persistently and consistently pursuing value and validation in the struggle. Insodoing, he gives this gift to ultra runners by designing races that tease this out of every participant.

Presented by Kiprun.

Watch Part 1 and Part 2 of the Laz Lake Interviews.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Lazarus Lake interview [Pt 2] | the Last Rebel of Ultra Running
item.itunes:title: Lazarus Lake interview [Pt 2] | the Last Rebel of Ultra Running
item.description:
Gary Cantrell (aka Lazarus Lake) is the last rebel of ultra running.

Watch our conversation on Youtube.

We love the professionalization of trail running but we don't want it all to go that way. In order for that to be interesing, the world of underground ultra running has to live strong.

In this episode, Josh works through the stories of Popcorn Sutton and Andy Payne with Laz.

Popcorn Sutton was a moonshiner untouched by modernization. He stood in stark contrast to the Jack Daniels and Jim Beams of the world. They work to maximize revenue and total annual output where Popcorn kept doing things the same way.

Andy Payne won the Bunion Derby in 1928 - a race across America with 275 participants. He also happened to be Laz's Dad's neighbor.

Laz is a blend of the two figures with a rock and roll-type status preserving a type of heart of running all its own.

Enjoy.

Presented by Kiprun.

----

Listen to Scene One (the Races of Laz Lake) on Spotify or Apple or watch on YouTube.

----

  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter

item.title: Lazarus Lake interview [Pt 1] | the Future of Barkleys + Ultrarunning
item.itunes:title: Lazarus Lake interview [Pt 1] | the Future of Barkleys + Ultrarunning
item.description:
Josh flew to Bell Buckle, TN to interview Lazarus Lake (aka Gary Cantrell) at his home and backdrop to Big's (and Little's) Backyard Ultra.

There are three scenes to The Lazarus Lakes Interviews and in this first one they discuss the races. You all know about Barkleys Marathons and the Backyard Ultras but what about the other races like the Vol State 500k or the now deceased Idiot's Run? Or ARFTA (a race for the ages)?

To anyone who knows of Laz Lake, you will not be surprised to learn that this interview was a wild ride in and of itself. He rarely speaks concretely, almost everything is metaphorical. And once you accept that you understand that he's rarely simply talking about a tree or a cave or Jesse James' gold.

Josh went into this interview planning to uncover a PT Barnum type or an artist standing at a canvas. Instead, he found a scientist in a laboratory whose lab rats are actually ultrarunners.

He's tinkering with variables looking to uncover elements of the human spirt on one hand and on the other, he's at face value.

He's a country boy born in Texas and raised in Oklahoma and Tennessee.

Presented by Kiprun.

With support from Like the Wind Magazine + Hyperlyte Liquid Performance.

----

Listen to Scene Two with Laz (the Last Rebel of Ultra Running) on Spotify or Apple or watch on YouTube

For behind the scenes and uncut content from this interview, go to our Patreon.

----

item.title: Dean Karnazes | Is Ultra Running Still Just a Pair of Shoes and Place to Run?
item.itunes:title: Dean Karnazes | Is Ultra Running Still Just a Pair of Shoes and Place to Run?
item.description:
Dean Karnazes brings light to the world through running. Dean's books and myth motivated many to start running for the first time or pushed them to run further.

He's been an amassador for running the world over and on a scale no other runner has enjoyed. In this episode we dive into his appearances on Conan O'briend, David Letterman, and the ESPYs.

When he started running it was much simpler. It was really just a pair of shoes and a place to run. But that's not necessarily the good old days. Today there are more people running than ever. And because foundational he believes that running is a gift to all who do it, the growth in every aspect of the industry is net good.

Presented by Kiprun.

----

  1. Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  2. Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  3. Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  4. Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Scott Jurek | Has Ultra Running Become Too Polished?
item.itunes:title: Scott Jurek | Has Ultra Running Become Too Polished?
item.description:
Scott Jurek represents a generation of ultratrail runners who either had day jobs or were true dirtbag trail runners. Scott and Josh Rosenthal dive into the 'good old days' of trail running when a lot of the gear was DIY like punk rock or skateboarding.

Presented by Kiprun.

  • Scott Jurek and Y2K
  • DIY: Ultrarunners, Punk Rock, and Skateboarding
  • Runners have it easier with gear advances
  • Athlete mentality is shifting in a sterile direction
  • Mental resilience in ultra running
  • The lasting bonds of competing in ultratrail
  • The commercialization of Ultratrail Running
  • Despite changes, the essence of running remains accessible to everyone.

This conversation explores the evolution of ultra running, reflecting on personal experiences from the Y2K era to the present day. Josh Rosenthal and Scott Jurek discuss the changes in gear, the competitive landscape, and the importance of community within the sport. They emphasize the mental resilience required for ultra running and celebrate the friendships formed through shared experiences. Despite the commercialization of the sport, the essence of running remains accessible and fulfilling for all.

----

  • Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  • Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  • Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  • Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Nils Arend | The Speed Project Founder
item.itunes:title: Nils Arend | The Speed Project Founder
item.description:
Nils Arend started The Speed Project and it continues to bring light into the world through running.

[Presented by Kiprun]

Josh and Nils sit on a Rugby Field in Albi, FR at midnight to talk about the origin story of TSP and what it means to so many.

Yes, TSP is an ultrarunning event but its not an 'ultrarunning' event. Its not in the ultrarunning zeitgeist, its a zeitgeist all its own.

----

item.title: Allie Gibbons | 300 Miles at 22 Weeks Pregnant
item.itunes:title: Allie Gibbons | 300 Miles at 22 Weeks Pregnant
item.description:
Allie Gibbons ran The Speed Project in the Atacama Desert while 22 weeks pregnant. Prior to that, she ran Bigfoot 200 in the Washington wilderness after just learning that she was pregnant.

Presented by Kiprun.

In this conversation, they discuss the challenges of ultra running, the unique experience of being pregnant during such a demanding race, and the mental and physical preparations required.

Allie shares insights on health monitoring, the emotional waves experienced during long races, and the camaraderie found within the ultra running community. The conversation also touches on the transition from road to trail running and the competitive spirit that drives athletes in these extreme conditions.

----

  • Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  • Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  • Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  • Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: For DIE HARD Trail Running Shoe Fans
item.itunes:title: For DIE HARD Trail Running Shoe Fans
item.description:
Josh Rosenthal and Inky Steve explore the future of running gear, discussing innovations in shoe design, the impact of cultural elements like movies on running, and the competitive landscape of brands in the industry.

[Presented by Kiprun]

They share insights from the TRE event, highlight the rise of smaller brands, and reflect on their experiences with various running products, including the Naked belt. The discussion emphasizes the importance of community and personal preferences in the running world.

Brands discussed:

  • Norda
  • Kiprun
  • Speedland
  • Nike / acg
  • Merrell
  • Salomon
  • Tantrums
  • SAYSKY
  • Mount to Coast

----

item.title: Trail Runner-ish, Light the World through Running, & The Next Great French Running Brand
item.itunes:title: Trail Runner-ish, Light the World through Running, & The Next Great French Running Brand
item.description:
I still consider myself a trail runner even though I never run on trails. I'm trail runner-ish.

There are a lot of people bringing light into the world through running. Let's talk about a few of them - Tommy Lewis, Josh Lynott, Nils Arend, Dean Karnazes, and more.

Presented by Kiprun.

Yes, we're presented by Kiprun but I also think they are the next big running brand coming out of France. Salomon and Hoka both started here. If Kiprun build momentum and iterate/improve year after year on the portfolio they just released, they will be in the brass ring.

----

  • Borderlands.cc | Podcast Network
  • Sign up: analog*Borderlands [new]letter
  • Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.
  • Salt Lake Foothills Trail Races | 30 May 2026

item.title: Running Recovery Slides & The Big Lebowski w/ Inky Steve
item.itunes:title: Running Recovery Slides & The Big Lebowski w/ Inky Steve
item.description:
The Dude Abides and would probably never wear trail running shoes. But we love this movie too much so Josh asked Inky, what shoes go best with The Big Lebowski? Slides. Super chill running recover slides.

There's a boom in them right now. You see them popping up all over the place and there's actually some innovation happening.

But at the end of the day, The Dude would wear the hell out of some recover slides and we have a blast exploring it.

----

item.title: What Happens When You Stop Using Strava?
item.itunes:title: What Happens When You Stop Using Strava?
item.description:
I stopped using strava and found 7 bugs that ended up being features.

[Borderlands TRE event - RSVP here]

Here are the bugs.

Listen to find out how these became features.

  1. I don't run as far
  2. I don't run as fast
  3. I can't review my performance
  4. I can't reminsce on old runs
  5. I can't be impressive
  6. I'm losing structure
  7. I don't have receipts for my runs

Presented by Naked Sports Innovations

----

item.title: a Mount to Coast Deep Dive with Taylor Bodin
item.itunes:title: a Mount to Coast Deep Dive with Taylor Bodin

item.title: Running Gave Ian Morgan Life, Took It Away, and Gave it Back Again
item.itunes:title: Running Gave Ian Morgan Life, Took It Away, and Gave it Back Again
item.description:
I've never interviewed someone so at peace with themselves. This interview with Ian Morgan felt real and honest and kind. There's something in his eyes that I think we all want.

[Presented by Naked Sports Innovations]

In this interview we talk about his relationship with alcohol, the sedentary life, and the way he left it behind.

He also died once while running and came back to life to tell the story.

Take this episode with you on a run to find inspiration to keep going.

item.title: 40,000 Kids in East African Run Clubs
item.itunes:title: 40,000 Kids in East African Run Clubs
item.description:
Brett Harrison started Red Knot Racing Co. as an expression of his heart for Tanzania, Tanzanians, and running.  His first mission, however, is not running.  It's community development.

[Presented by Naked Sports Innovations]

Brett moved to Tanzania in 2009 where, among other things, he helped improve agricultral practices.  But over time, his staff grew to 15 local Tanzanians who started putting on endurance events at the base of Kilimanjaro and to this day is the only organization permitted to do them year after year.

But again, its not about the races.  Races are a means of community development.

To an outsider, you see world class events and assume he's building something to sell to UTMB or building an empire for empire's sake.  He's not.  He's building something to positively impact the lives of Tanzanians.

By the way, Red Knot puts on the largest outdoor sports festival in East Africa with 1,500 attendees.

Check out Red Knot Racing and Red Knot Development. 

----

item.title: Jason Schlarb, Elite Runner and Norda Team Manager
item.itunes:title: Jason Schlarb, Elite Runner and Norda Team Manager
item.description:
Jason Schlarb started his running career ~15 years ago when the frontier of ultrarunning was much less developed.

[Presented by Naked Sports Innovations]

Today, while still running at an elite level himself, Jason is managing the Norda athlete team in a way that was impossible through most of his career.

Josh and Jason had a chat in a loud restaurant after a hard (for Josh) run in Annecy, France celebrating the Norda 005s along with French running store Quatorze.

item.title: Rocky Raccoon 100 | Where Roots Come Alive [trail icons: Texas]
item.itunes:title: Rocky Raccoon 100 | Where Roots Come Alive [trail icons: Texas]
item.description:
Rocky Raccoon is an iconic rooty and rootsy East Texas race that many great runners run as a right of passage.

Presented by Naked Sports Innovations

Jordan rightfully places Rocky Raccoon 100 in the upper eschelon of Texas Trail ultrarunning races comparing its endless loops to Lightnin' Hopkins and the endless 12 bar blues.

Thanks to our special guests: Zach Bitter, Joe Prusaitis, PJ Izaguirre, Brandon Dutra, and Chris McWatters.

item.title: Caleb Bowen & Javelina Jundred | Revenge After a 14:20 Finish Last Year
item.itunes:title: Caleb Bowen & Javelina Jundred | Revenge After a 14:20 Finish Last Year
item.description:
Caleb Bowen, XC Coach at Marshall University, has been running at a high level for a long time. He's part of the crew putting West Virginia on the map (the Bargain Boys).

Presented by Naked Sports Innovations

In the lead up to Javelina Jundred, Caleb talks about last years 14:20 finish and how he thinks he can make up time on it.

He took M1 at Hurricane 100k last month and now looks to bring his fitness and experience back to Javelina Jundred 2025.

item.title: Careth Arnold & Javelina Jundred | 2025 Wins at TDS, Twisted Fork, and Hurricane 100k
item.itunes:title: Careth Arnold & Javelina Jundred | 2025 Wins at TDS, Twisted Fork, and Hurricane 100k
item.description:
Careth Arnold is having an all time great season capped by a win at TDS at UTMB where she became the first American winner of TDS, a super hard and technical race.

[Presented by Naked Sports Innovations]

9 days later she ran and won Hurricane 100k, a much more runnable course than TDS proving shes got the fitness and speed necessary to toe the line at Javelina Jundred this week.

item.title: Can You Beat Mount to Coast H1s for the Price?
item.itunes:title: Can You Beat Mount to Coast H1s for the Price?
item.description:
The Mount to Coast H1s are affordable, well-designed, and durable. The problem? Inventory. They've rolled these out so well that they're hard to get in some markets.

Presented by Naked Sports Innovations

Inky and Josh jump in talk H1s, Hoka's revival of the Speedgoat 2s, Inkys run for the homeless of Manchester, pricing strategy, and more.

Are the Mount to Coast H1s a competitor of the Norda 005?

We explore.

Borderlands.cc review of the H1s.

item.title: From Scrubbing Toilets to Running Ultras: Biel
item.itunes:title: From Scrubbing Toilets to Running Ultras: Biel
item.description:
Mike Krazter wrote an article about Biel where he said "Berlin runs on extremes, and Biel is one of its secret engines."

Biel runs far and fast and often on the streets of Berlin training for the 24 Hour World Championships where he will run for his home country of Brazil.

[Presented by Naked Sports Innovations]

He ran TSP solo and unsupported from Chamonix to Marseille (find out in this episode why he didn't finish).

item.title: Finish in a Good Place | UNNA and the Heart of Running
item.itunes:title: Finish in a Good Place | UNNA and the Heart of Running
item.description:
Presented by Naked Sports Innovations

John-Ruben started UNNA. This Stockholm-based brand represents the heart and soul of running beyond speed and competition. They say "it's not about finishing in first place, it's about finishing in a good place."

I can not explain it full here. I ask that if I've earned your trust in the last few years that you would simply press play and enjoy it.

Check out the UNNA x HOKA collab.

item.title: TheROCKER by Satisfy is Fear & Loathing in Las Vegas
item.itunes:title: TheROCKER by Satisfy is Fear & Loathing in Las Vegas
item.description:
Inky and Josh have been comparing iconic trail running shoes to iconic movies and today's will be hard to beat now and forever more. We look at Hunter S. Thompson, theROCKER by Satisfy Running, and Fear + Loathing in Las Vegas.

Satisy appeals to the myth of the west and the american dream inherent within it. This is nothing if not Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas.

Enjoy.

item.title: theMAMMOTH 200 + Jimmy Elam, the Chico State Legend
item.itunes:title: theMAMMOTH 200 + Jimmy Elam, the Chico State Legend
item.description:
Jimmy Elam has been running big for years. He's won 1st at Cascade Crest, High Lonesome, Wasatch 100, the Bear, the Canyons, and more.

And now he toes the line at theMAMMOTH 200 ready to compete with other greats like Tara Dower, Max Jolliffe, Rachel Entrekin, Timmy Olson, and more.

It's an inaugural year for theMAMMOTH and it will be hard to beat it in future years with this line up.

item.title: Tim Tollefson + Hilary Yang | theMAMMOTH 200
item.itunes:title: Tim Tollefson + Hilary Yang | theMAMMOTH 200
item.description:
Tim Tollefson started Mammoth Trailfest and this year, with the help of Hilary Yang, he's adding a 200 miler after the fest ends call theMAMMOTH. It's quite an accomplishment to wrangle a 200 miler and add to that a marquee year one field with names like Tara Dower, Rachel Entrekin, Max Jolliffe, Jimmy Elam, Jeff Browning, Tim Olson and more.

In this episode we discuss the $20,000 prize purse, livestream and build general hype for what has all the ingredients to be a great race for years to come.

Elite Ultras Should Pay Prize Money

Join me at the International Running Expo - (IRX)

item.title: Joe Corcione | The Power of Belief: Achieving the Impossible in Ultra Running
item.itunes:title: Joe Corcione | The Power of Belief: Achieving the Impossible in Ultra Running
item.description:
In this episode of Full Price Athlete with Kade Wilcox, Joe Corcione shares his transformative journey from battling addiction to becoming an ultra-endurance athlete. He discusses the pivotal role of passion in his success, the mental fortitude required for ultra marathons, and the importance of having a purpose greater than the pain. Joe also offers practical advice for aspiring ultra runners, emphasizing the significance of belief and resilience in achieving seemingly impossible goals.

item.title: Bandera | No Whiners, Wimps, or Wusses [trail icons: Texas]
item.itunes:title: Bandera | No Whiners, Wimps, or Wusses [trail icons: Texas]
item.description:
Welcome to Trail Icons: Texas a new series by Jordan Gracey for Borderlands.

Jordan delves into the Bandera 100k (and Cactus Rose) in Texas, exploring their history, challenges, and unique aspects. Featuring insights from race organizers and participants, it highlights what makes these races special in the trail running community.

In this episode, Jordan features Joe Prusaitis (Bandera Founder), Brad Quin, Zach Bitter, Erik Stanley (founder of Trail Roots).

Expect to learn about the history of a Trail Icon: Bandera from the founders and iconic contributors to its success over the past years.

item.title: Dead Last at Wasatch 100
item.itunes:title: Dead Last at Wasatch 100
item.description:
Emma Giebler ran Wasatch this passed weekend and got dead last. Three years ago she ran and got 2nd to last (dead last female).

[SILO | Crew Drop One - the best ultra drop bag. Code Borderlands for 10% off.]

In this episode Josh (host) and Emma relive the 2022 running of the Wasatch 100 where they met at Ant Knolls Aid Station when Emma looked totally and complete done. She popped and kept going.

Brilliant and inspiring.

item.title: Managing Sleep, Nutrition, + Overcoming Alcohol Addiction | Abraham Bejil + Edgar Lazoya
item.itunes:title: Managing Sleep, Nutrition, + Overcoming Alcohol Addiction | Abraham Bejil + Edgar Lazoya
item.description:
Join host Kade Wilcox (Full Price Athlete: spotify | apple) as he delves into the world of ultra running with guests Abraham Bejil and Edgar Lozoya. Discover the journeys of these two athletes as they prepare for the Moab 240, sharing insights on training, overcoming personal challenges, and the dedication required to pursue their passion.

  • Abraham's transition from battling alcohol addiction to becoming an ultra runner and coach.
  • Edgar's journey from a 5K with his son to tackling the Moab 240. The importance of speed work and race compounding in ultra training.
  • Strategies for managing sleep and nutrition during long races.
  • The mental and physical challenges of ultra running and how to overcome them.

Don't miss this episode filled with motivation and practical tips for aspiring ultra runners.

Subscribe to the Full Price Athlete podcast and follow the journey of these incredible athletes.

item.title: Doug Mayer | Tor des Géants, Run the Alps, + the Race that Changed Running
item.itunes:title: Doug Mayer | Tor des Géants, Run the Alps, + the Race that Changed Running
item.description:
Doug Mayer joined Josh in Chamonix as UTMB was in full swing to talk about his new book the Last of the Giants. It's a great read to enjoy alone or with your kids surprisingly as the illustrations are breathtaking and accessible.

We also talked about Doug's previous book: the Race that Changed Running about the short history of UTMB.

Not to mention his iconic Run the Alps enterprise bringing runners from all over the world to enjoy brilliant runs in the unmatched beauty of the alps.

They explore the soulful side of endurance running and talk about the value of UTMB and TSP to endurance runners.

item.description:
Allison Baca runs for Hoka (Mafate 5's out now) and will toe the line at CCC next week and the World Championships in late September.

item.title: UTMB Addresses Our Travel Fee/Carbon Fee Concerns
item.itunes:title: UTMB Addresses Our Travel Fee/Carbon Fee Concerns
item.description:
Fabrice Perrin, UTMB's Chief of Sport and Sustainability, heard our Bad Runners Take, where we largely celebrated UTMB but also critiqued the new mandatory Travel Fee for 2026, initially applied to runners traveling to the Chamonix Main Event.

UTMB creates strong demand for their event, only to impose a variable fee that increases with travel distance, impacting runners coming from farther away.

Is it greenwashing? Fabrice responds.

----

item.title: Explained: Big Mountain Running Nutrition for 50k to 250 Miles
item.itunes:title: Explained: Big Mountain Running Nutrition for 50k to 250 Miles
item.description:
How do you plan for big mountain races at high altitude with lots of climbing. It's UTMB + Leadville season so we're looking at those races and thinking through how best to approach them.

Jesse advises on nutrition for everything from 50k to 200+ milers in this episode.

Jesse Rich of Nutrition Rundown is a coach and nutritionist based in Salt Lake City. He also races ultras with a 1st place win at Wasatch 100, 2nd at Bear 100, and fresh off 11th at a CRAZY competitive Speedgoat.

item.title: UTMB: Great But Facing Critical Challenges | Bad Runners Take
item.itunes:title: UTMB: Great But Facing Critical Challenges | Bad Runners Take
item.description:
Josh + Brian welcome Sam Lohse to bring a new perspective to today's Bad Runners Take.

In this episode we answer two questions and take issue.

  1. What should America ultra trail races adopt from UTMB?
  2. What shoudl UTMB adopt from America?
  3. What do we think of UTMB's new climate fees introduced in 2026?

We also get a call on the Hyperlyte Hotline about running and shaving body hair.

  • 00:00 Introduction to Bad Runners Take
  • 02:34 UTMB vs. American Ultra Running: A Comparative Discussion
  • 07:40 The Dirtbag Culture in Ultra Running
  • 15:23 What UTMB Can Learn from American Races
  • 22:19 Environmental Concerns and the Future of UTMB
  • 24:25 Carbon Offsetting in Sports Events
  • 27:27 Critique of Greenwashing and Corporate Responsibility
  • 31:25 The Role of Individual Responsibility in Climate Action
  • 36:30 The Need for Systemic Change in Environmental Policies
  • 37:23 Celebrating the Spirit of Trail Running

(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

----

item.title: The Hardrock 100: A Run not a Race? It's a Race. And a Run. [Bad Runners Take]
item.itunes:title: The Hardrock 100: A Run not a Race? It's a Race. And a Run. [Bad Runners Take]
item.description:
In this episode, Josh and Brian dive deep into the Hard Rock ultramarathon, discussing their experiences, reflections on the race, and the community surrounding it.

  • Josh reflects on his evolving understanding of Hard Rock.
  • The Hardrock race is seen as more of a celebration than a competition.
  • Katie's course record
  • The Here for the Women's Race shirt incident sparks a conversation about intentions and perceptions.
  • Outrider Running League is gaining traction with signups.
  • Listener engagement through the Hyperlite hotline is encouraged.
  • Nike's investment in trail running could change the landscape of the sport.
  • The willingness to adapt and change opinions is crucial in discussions.

item.itunes:title: What's Special About Hardrock 100? Hyperlyte Hotline, Outrider Running League, Dan Green/Max Jolliffe/Wolfie Run [Bad Runners Take]
item.description:
Hardrock 100 comes quickly after Western States perhaps hiding in a shadow it prefers. More than any other ultramarathon, Hardrock is about the runner. Other notable races have to navigate a lot of pomp and circumstance, but this one focuses on getting 145 runner around the loop to kiss the rock.

So what's so special about the Hardrock 100? Find out.

item.title: Paris Fashion Week + Run Culture - what's the connection? + PFW Recap
item.itunes:title: Paris Fashion Week + Run Culture - what's the connection? + PFW Recap
item.description:
Paris Fashion Week is a celebration of running and style. Innovative running brands show up from all over the world to showoff upcoming styles, run together, dine together, and generally engage the aggressively growing running indsutry.

Huge thanks to Aire Libre + Naked Sports Innovations for the support necessary to make this all happen.

We went to see a lot of brands. Here's the skinny on each one:

item.title: Who is Going to Win? Pre Western States Race Coverage: Bad Runners Take
item.itunes:title: Who is Going to Win? Pre Western States Race Coverage: Bad Runners Take
item.description:
Josh Rosenthal and Wolfie dive deep into the upcoming Western States 100.

They discuss the dynamics between established elite runners ('Made Men') and emerging talents ('Associates'), the impact of age on performance, and the significance of prize money in the sport.

The conversation also highlights the competitive landscape of the women's race and the potential for community engagement to shape the future of ultra running.

With insights on key runners like Killian Jornet, Hans, and Dan Green, this episode is a must-listen for ultra running enthusiasts.

item.title: Hans Troyer/The Kid, WSER Hype, Pure Running at Paris Fashion Week [Bad Runners Take]
item.itunes:title: Hans Troyer/The Kid, WSER Hype, Pure Running at Paris Fashion Week [Bad Runners Take]
item.description:
Josh Rosenthal and Wolfie discuss the upcoming Western States race, featuring elite athlete interviews, insights into the competition, and a unique connection to Paris Fashion Week.

They explore the human interest aspects of running, the significance of community engagement through the Hyperlite Hotline, and the impact of documentaries like Hans Troyer's on the running culture.

The conversation emphasizes the importance of running beyond just events, celebrating the joy and expression of running itself. The running movement of Paris Fashion Week ironically celebrates this better than any other.

(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

item.title: Is Norda 001 the Air Max 1 of running? + Mallrats [Inky Steve]
item.itunes:title: Is Norda 001 the Air Max 1 of running? + Mallrats [Inky Steve]
item.description:
Manchester's favorite running child, Inky Steve, joins Josh again to was poetic on the intersection of Trail Culture x Sneaker Culture. We also pick a movie to characterize the intersection of the day.

Air Max 1s are iconic and we believe Norda 001s have the same potential to be unchanged for decades to come.

Did Nike know they had a winner from day one? Did Norda?

Time will tell, but there are signs we look for to prove the 001 is already a cult classic along with Air Max 1 + Mallrats.

(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

item.title: Lydia Oldham | 650km Projecto Portugal, Solo TSP, and How to Savor Big Accomplishments
item.itunes:title: Lydia Oldham | 650km Projecto Portugal, Solo TSP, and How to Savor Big Accomplishments
item.description:
Lydia Oldham ran across Portugal (5 days, 17 hours) and did a solo TSP from Las Angeles to Las Vegas. Runners like Lydia almost make it look easy so long as you don't pause and contemplate the wild complexity of planning and near-impossible reality of actually executing it physically.

Josh is starting to get the bug.

item.title: From 50 Miles to Moab 240 with Kade Wilcox
item.itunes:title: From 50 Miles to Moab 240 with Kade Wilcox
item.description:
Kade Wilcox perfectly represents the original audience of Borderlands.

Even the name Borderlands means the land between two places - your daily life and your amobitious ultrarunning goals.

Kade is spinning a ton of plates, paying full price for gear and jumping from his furthest run of 50 miles to 240 miles.

  1. Kade Wilcox is transitioning from 50 miles to Moab 240.
  2. Balancing family, work, and training is crucial for ultra runners.
  3. Mental toughness is as important as physical training.
  4. Visualizing success can enhance performance.
  5. Mental reps during training are essential for preparation.

(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

---- Partners ----

item.title: Made Men vs the Associates, Haves vs Have Nots, Established vs Disruptors, WSER 2025 Narratives [Wolfie's World]
item.itunes:title: Made Men vs the Associates, Haves vs Have Nots, Established vs Disruptors, WSER 2025 Narratives [Wolfie's World]
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

Wolfie lays out the story lines for WSER 2025.

the Made men vs. the Associates. Made men are official, initiated Mafia members with full status, while associates work with the Mafia but lack initiation and privileges.

He's looking at the runners who are made vs. the ones trying to make it.

Kilian Jornet vs. Hans Troyer

Dan Jones vs. Seth Ruhling

Rod Farvard vs. Vincent Bouillard

Adam Peterman vs. Caleb Olson

David Roche vs. David Roche

Wolfie explains it.

item.title: Will Western States Records Fall in 2025? [States Debates]
item.itunes:title: Will Western States Records Fall in 2025? [States Debates]
item.description:
The male and female course records at Western States are stout.

Will the current course records held by Jim Walmsley (14:09 in 2019) and Courtney Dauwalter (15:29 in 2023) respectively fall during the 2025 running of WSER?

As a fan, we just want a great race regardless of times, but both fields are poised to attack the CRs.

If only one CR falls, whose is most likely to go? Courtney's or Jim's?

Joe Corcione of Everyday Ultra debates Brian Peterson on today's episode.

(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

item.title: [Bad Runners Take] Max Jolliffe/Trail Earnhardt, BPN's Backyard, a Dirtbag Race, WSER Hype
item.itunes:title: [Bad Runners Take] Max Jolliffe/Trail Earnhardt, BPN's Backyard, a Dirtbag Race, WSER Hype
item.description:
In this episode of the Bad Runners Take on the Borderlands Trail and Ultra Running Podcast, Josh + Brian discuss various topics including the evolution of Max Jolliffe's new nickname, reflections on recent dirtbag races, + more.

  1. Max's new nickname: Trail Earnhardt
  2. Experiencing a race IRL reignites Josh's passion for the sport.
  3. Written training logs enhance reflection and mental clarity.
  4. Borderlands' WSER coverage will supplement not replace
  5. Highlighting the sub-24 hour challenge is crucial for engaging fans.
  6. BPN's event showcased exceptional hospitality and engagement.
  7. Celebrating achievements in running should be prioritized over controversies.

item.title: [Wolfie's World] Present Challenges Create Your Future Self
item.itunes:title: [Wolfie's World] Present Challenges Create Your Future Self
item.description:
In this episode of Wolfie's World, Brian discusses the recent controversies in the trail running community, emphasizing the importance of focusing on the collective strength of the 95.

He explores how to endure pain and suffering during ultra races, highlighting the significance of mental resilience and the concept of the future self.

Brian shares insights on training strategies that build mental toughness and encourages listeners to embrace challenges as part of their journey in ultra running.

item.title: Speedland, Vans, + High Fidelity: Trail Running Shoes x Sneaker Culture
item.itunes:title: Speedland, Vans, + High Fidelity: Trail Running Shoes x Sneaker Culture
item.description:
Manchester's own Inky Steve joins Josh to talk about the intersection of trail running shoes and sneaker culture.

It's best couched in a quote from High Fidelity - "What really matters is what you like, not what you are like."

We wear the shoes that we like and most often we choose based on looks. We run in them, we like how they look and thus like how we look. And we run better, whatever that means.

(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

item.title: Made Men vs. Associates: Western States 100 2025
item.itunes:title: Made Men vs. Associates: Western States 100 2025
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

In this conversation, Josh + Brian discuss their ambitious plans for Western States coverage, focusing on unique storytelling and the dynamics between established elite runners (made men) and emerging talents (associates).

They explore the challenges faced by runners balancing day jobs with their passion for ultra running, and the importance of highlighting underappreciated stories within the community.

The discussion also delves into the recent Aravaipa controversy, examining community reactions, the need for transparency, and the existing divide within the ultra running community.

In this conversation, the hosts delve into the complexities of the trail running community, discussing the reactions to recent events, the importance of discipline, and the need for clear rules in racing.

item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

In this episode of Wolfie's World, host Brian discusses the importance of consistency in ultra running, upcoming events, and the ongoing debate about prize money in the sport.

He emphasizes the significance of fun in running, encouraging the 95 to remember why they started and to keep the joy alive in their training and racing.

The conversation also touches on community, personal growth, and the need to uplift others within the ultra running community.

item.title: Born to Run author, Chris McDougall - Caballo Blanco, Barefoot Ted, and the Rest of the Gang.
item.itunes:title: Born to Run author, Chris McDougall - Caballo Blanco, Barefoot Ted, and the Rest of the Gang.
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

Chris McDougall wrote Born to Run and today we get the story straight from him. We hear some updates on the characters from the book and hear some stories behind the stories.

We go beyond just Born to Run though - we talk my life-changing books - Born to Run, My Name is Asher Lev, Catcher in the Rye, and more.

This was a rich conversation that we had in March of 2024. It was such a special conversation that I didn't want it to live in the archives forever. I need to bring it back from time to time.

Ultrarunning owes Chris a great deal of gratitude for his contributions.

---- Partners ----

item.title: Wolfie's World: Pressure of Racing, Gym Bro Ultrarunners, + Balancing Ego
item.itunes:title: Wolfie's World: Pressure of Racing, Gym Bro Ultrarunners, + Balancing Ego
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

In this episode of Wolfie's World, Brian (wolffrunner) discusses the dynamic nature of the Borderlands community, upcoming debates, and the pressures faced by elite runners.

He delves into the concept of ego, consistency, and self-esteem in ultra running, emphasizing the importance of community engagement and the balance of ego within the sport.

Brian encourages listeners to embrace challenges and foster connections, ultimately aiming for personal growth and a stronger community.

  1. Upcoming debates will focus on rivalries in ultra running.
  2. Elite racing brings significant pressure and expectations.
  3. Ego can be both a challenge and a tool in ultra running.
  4. Consistency is key to building self-esteem and success.
  5. The ultra community should welcome all, including gym bros.
  6. Community engagement fosters a supportive environment.

---- Partners ----

item.title: Bad Runners Take - Cocodona 250 Coverage, Hardrock Gatekeeping, WSER: Mademen vs. the Rest
item.itunes:title: Bad Runners Take - Cocodona 250 Coverage, Hardrock Gatekeeping, WSER: Mademen vs. the Rest
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

In this episode, Josh + Brian tend to some popular topics in their own unique way.

There's a lot to celebrate about Cocodona 250. Many podcasts have already named those things. In our post-race analysis we look at the coverage and think through how it could be better even with the existing budget.

Karl Meltzer joined us last week and dropped a lot of gold. Too much acutally. We needed this episode to follow up on some of his takes and drill down on them including his take Hardrock and UTMB.

Western States - as we start to shape our own narratives of note, wolfie begins to frame what he thinks is most interesting: mademen vs. the rest, haves vs. the have nots.

---- Partners ----

item.title: Karl Speedgoat Meltzer Joins the STATES DEBATES: Nike vs. Hoka
item.itunes:title: Karl Speedgoat Meltzer Joins the STATES DEBATES: Nike vs. Hoka
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

In this episode, the Josh hosts Brian Peterson (Wolfie's World) + Karl Speedgoat Meltzer to dbate about the sponsorship of Western States, focusing on the merits of Nike versus Hoka as potential partners.

This is hypothetical, obviously. We use Nike to make a point - Hoka is missing a lot of opportunities that Nike would not miss.

Ultimately, this feels like less of a debate and more of a discussion around what we need in this sport.

Both sides present compelling arguments, with insights from Karl Meltzer, a prominent figure in the trail running community (and Hoka's first pro athlete and namesake of one of the best selling shoes in trail running history - Hoka Speedgoat), emphasizing the importance of sponsorship dynamics and media representation in shaping the future of ultra running.

item.itunes:title: Big Mountain Trail Running Shoes: Arc'Teryx, La Sportiva, Rossignol
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

Josh and Taylor Bodin (Believe in the Run, Dirt Division) discuss the evolution and innovations of three prominent big mountain brands in the trail running space: La Sportiva, Arc'teryx, and Rossignol.

They explore how these brands, known for their heritage in mountain sports, are adapting their technologies and designs to appeal to the North American market.

The conversation highlights specific shoe models, their performance characteristics, and the brands' strategies for breaking into the competitive trail running market.

item.description:

Cocodona 250 kicks off on May 5 and we're ready but we need a rivalry in this race.

Finn Melanson vs Max Jolliffe - why not?

Wolffrunner makes the case of why Finn vs. Max makes sense -two great 200 miler runners in their prime.

But it goes further than that. Listen for to find out.

Also, a look at Dan Green (Josh's pick for M1, overall 2), Courtney (overall 1), Michael McKnight (the winningest 200 miler to date) and more.

item.title: Facing Darkness: The Psychology of Ultrarunning + the Goggins Effect [Wolfie's World]
item.itunes:title: Facing Darkness: The Psychology of Ultrarunning + the Goggins Effect [Wolfie's World]
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

Wolfie talks about the 'Goggins effect'—the need to channel inner strength and confront personal insecurities during races.

  1. Ultra running is increasingly seen as a social flex, similar to marathons years ago.
  2. Mental preparation is as crucial as physical training for ultra races.
  3. Runners must confront their insecurities during 100-mile races.
  4. The 'Goggins pill' symbolizes the need for inner strength during tough moments.
  5. Many runners may not be physically prepared for the demands of a 100-miler.
  6. Adversity in life can prepare runners for the challenges of ultra races.

item.title: English Ultrarunning with Inky Steve
item.itunes:title: English Ultrarunning with Inky Steve
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

Josh joined Inky Steve in Manchester, England for run, hair cut, and podcast recording about the soul and spirit of UK running.

This conversation explores the journey of running, from the punk rock spirit that inspires runners to the community support that uplifts them.

The speakers share personal experiences with marathons, the evolution of running shoes, and the impact of COVID on their running journeys.

They discuss the importance of mental toughness, personal goals, and celebrating small victories along the way. In this conversation, the speakers discuss the significance of participating in races for training, the emergence of Twisted Running as a community, the latest innovations in running shoes, and the balance between function and design in footwear.

They also share personal experiences and upcoming race goals, emphasizing the camaraderie and excitement within the running community.

item.title: [Soar Running] Serendipity in Running + Design
item.itunes:title: [Soar Running] Serendipity in Running + Design
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

In this episode, Josh interviews Tim Soar, founder of Soar Running, exploring the intersection of fashion and performance in running apparel. Tim shares his journey from a failed fashion label to creating a brand that emphasizes both style and functionality.

The conversation delves into the philosophy of product development, the importance of innovation, and the distinction between luxury and ultra-premium products in the running gear market.

In this conversation, the speaker discusses the evolution of sportswear design, emphasizing the importance of tactile experience, emotional connection, and the role of failure in entrepreneurship.

They share personal anecdotes about navigating uncertainty, the serendipitous moments that shaped their brand, and the significance of aesthetics in performance wear.

The discussion also touches on the challenges of scaling creativity and the art of minimalism in design, culminating in a reflection on the journey of building a successful brand.

item.title: Trail Running is Supposed to Be Messy [Wolfie's World]
item.itunes:title: Trail Running is Supposed to Be Messy [Wolfie's World]
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

Pushed through the lens of the Arizona Monster 300 and the problematic PR around it, Wolfie concludes that trail running is supposed to be messy. These events are hard to put on.

We all forget that Cocodona was not perfect in year one.

Actual runners who are paying the fees don't care much about the controversy and good for them. These events are ultimately about the runners and not the peanut gallery anyways.

The success of events should be measured by runner satisfaction.

item.title: Pre-Race Nutrition for Ultramarathons
item.itunes:title: Pre-Race Nutrition for Ultramarathons
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

In this episode, Josh and Jesse Rich discuss the importance of nutrition for runners, particularly focusing on the week leading up to a race.

They explore strategies for carb loading, the significance of glycogen, and how to optimize race day nutrition. Jesse emphasizes the need to balance fiber intake and carbohydrate consumption to ensure optimal performance without gastrointestinal distress.

In this conversation, Jesse Rich and Josh discuss the critical role of nutrition in athletic performance, particularly in endurance sports. They explore carbohydrate loading, race nutrition strategies, and the importance of post-race recovery meals.

  1. The week before a race is crucial for nutrition, focusing on carb loading and fiber tapering.
  2. Glycogen is stored glucose that provides energy during races.
  3. Refined carbs are encouraged the week before a race to minimize fiber intake.
  4. A disciplined race nutrition strategy can prevent bonking and improve performance.

---- Partners ----

Wylder - Host and find Run Clubs, schedule public and private group runs

Naked Sports Innovations - the worlds best hydration belt

Salt Lake Footshills Trail Races. Salt Lake City, UT - May 31, 2025

item.title: Preparing to Run Further than Ever (+ the price)
item.itunes:title: Preparing to Run Further than Ever (+ the price)
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

Ryan Ramsby write 100+ content for Borderlands.cc - shout to SILO and its Crew Drop One bag for sponsoring 100+ content.

----

In this episode, Josh Rosenthal and Ryan Ramsby discuss the intricacies of ultra running, focusing on the preparation for events like Cocodona and Tahoe 200.

They explore the importance of gear, the costs associated with ultra running, and the mental challenges faced during long-distance races. Ryan shares his experiences and insights on essential gear, foot care, and the significance of being present during races.

In this conversation, Ryan shares insights on preparing for endurance races, particularly the Tahoe 200. He discusses sleep strategies, gear selection, and the importance of planning and logistics. The conversation also touches on upcoming gear reviews and the critical role of nutrition

  1. Preparing for ultra races can feel like starting from scratch.
  2. The cost of ultra running is often underestimated.
  3. Proper gear is crucial for success in ultra running.
  4. Foot care is essential to avoid injuries during races.
  5. Mental presence is key to enduring long races.
  6. Investing in quality gear can prevent race-day failures.

item.title: Every Runner's Journey is Valid and Inspiring [Wolfie's World]
item.itunes:title: Every Runner's Journey is Valid and Inspiring [Wolfie's World]
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android.)

In this episode of Wolfie's World on the Borderlands Podcast, host Brian shares a heartfelt message to the 95 community, emphasizing the importance of unity, dedication, and the shared journey of runners balancing their passion with everyday responsibilities.

He reflects on the collective strength of the 95 lifestyle, encouraging listeners to embrace their roles as inspirations to others, especially their children, and to celebrate their unique journeys without comparison to elite athletes.

  1. The 95 community is about balancing life and passion.
  2. Children look up to their parents' dedication.
  3. The collective 'we' is more powerful than individual achievements.
  4. The 95 lifestyle fosters a sense of belonging.
  5. Engagement and support within the community are vital.
  6. Celebrating small victories is important.
  7. The 95 is a diverse and inclusive community.

Hosted by Brian (Wolfrunner).

---- Partners ----

item.title: Is it Time to Welcome Camille Herron Back? [Bad Runners Take] on Trail Running Current Events
item.itunes:title: Is it Time to Welcome Camille Herron Back? [Bad Runners Take] on Trail Running Current Events
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android).

  1. Should we welcome Camille Herron back into the fold?
  2. There are 14.8 Million trail runners in America and maybe 50,000 who care about elite trail running events in some form or fashion. What do we do with this?
  3. Down with the trail running gatekeepers.

Cam Hanes' post that we referenced.

Bad Runners Take is a take on trail running current events - sometimes unique, counter, and other times cookie cutter. But we promise we always come to the episode trying to think honestly.

item.title: Trail Running for All: Past the Gates, Into the Wild
item.itunes:title: Trail Running for All: Past the Gates, Into the Wild
item.description:
(Wylder is now available on iOS + Android).

Gatekeepers can only stop us if we let them. Any one and every one is welcome on public trail to run however they want, in whatever gear they want, as slow as they want. They can walk the whole time and call themselves a trail runner.

Let's bust down the gates and let everyone in.

This is a paired down version of a much longer episode of Redefine the Game with Francisco Fonseca.

item.title: Bad Takes on Trail Running Current Events: Cam Hanes + Courtney, Max to BPN, AZ Monster vs Cocodona
item.itunes:title: Bad Takes on Trail Running Current Events: Cam Hanes + Courtney, Max to BPN, AZ Monster vs Cocodona
item.description:
[Wylder is now available on iOS]

Wolfie joins Josh on another Bad Runners Take where they look at current events and offer unique perspectives.

  1. Why does Courtney Dauwalter do more with Cam Haines than the expected trail running news outlets like Singletrack or Freetrail?
  2. Max Jolliffe goes to Bare Nutrition Products (BPN)
  3. Can the Arizona Monster dethrone Cocodona as the premier 200+ mile race
  4. Does the recent contraversy around Candice matter?

Our goal on these episodes is not to be critical. Rather, its to bring new perspectives to trail running in hopes of showing that more takes is better for the growing industry.

item.title: Borderlands: 3 Year Anniversary
item.itunes:title: Borderlands: 3 Year Anniversary
item.description:
In this special episode celebrating the three-year anniversary of Borderlands, I reflect on the journey of building a community around trail running.

I emphasize the importance of inclusivity in the sport, focusing on the heart of trail running rather than great performances. Josh introduces the Wylder app as a tool for fostering community among runners and discusses the challenges of monetization and brand partnerships.

  1. The heart of trail running is about participation, not competition.
  2. Wylder app aims to connect runners and build community.
  3. Brand partnerships can alter the authenticity of the message.
  4. Gratitude is expressed for the support from the Borderlands family.
  5. Future plans include expanding real-life community connections.

Wylder is currently only available on iOS. Android coming quickly.

item.content:encoded:
In this special episode celebrating the three-year anniversary of Borderlands, I reflect on the journey of building a community around trail running.

I emphasize the importance of inclusivity in the sport, focusing on the heart of trail running rather than great performances. Josh introduces the Wylder app as a tool for fostering community among runners and discusses the challenges of monetization and brand partnerships.

  1. The heart of trail running is about participation, not competition.
  2. Wylder app aims to connect runners and build community.
  3. Brand partnerships can alter the authenticity of the message.
  4. Gratitude is expressed for the support from the Borderlands family.
  5. Future plans include expanding real-life community connections.

Wylder is currently only available on iOS. Android coming quickly.

item.title: The Running Shoe Revolution You May Not Know About
item.itunes:title: The Running Shoe Revolution You May Not Know About
item.description:
Jurian Elstgeest founded 4T2 to put his own stamp on footwear and running culture. Read its manifesto and you'll see things like 'running is substance, not appearance' and 'running is made of rituals'.

4T2's community is small but growing.

It feels like there's a seed of this community in every major running market in the world.

*correction: 4T2 is HQed in Haarlem, not Amsterdam

item.title: Nike Trail Running: the Big Bad Wolf or Best of the Best?
item.itunes:title: Nike Trail Running: the Big Bad Wolf or Best of the Best?
item.description:
Taylor (lead trail reviewer of Believe in the Run) and I are long overdue on a Nike Trail Running episode.

They often get painted as the big bad wolf but today is not that kind of review. We're looking at their incredible trail team roster + analyzing their footwear philosophy and portfolio.

In this episode Josh learns there is such thing as a 'colorist' whose job is to create colorways. If you are a colorist, please join me on an episode.

  1. a complex history with both positive and negative perceptions.
  2. Investing in both ultra and sub-ultra trail running athletes.
  3. Nike's approach to innovation is often secretive, leading to speculation
  4. Nike's future in trail running looks promising.
  5. The trail running community benefits from Nike's presence

item.title: What are the Effects of Marijuana + Alcohol on Running?
item.itunes:title: What are the Effects of Marijuana + Alcohol on Running?
item.description:
Jesse Rich of Nutrition Rundown talks about the impact and effects of marijuana and alcohol on running.

  1. Alcohol affects nutrient absorption, particularly B vitamins.
  2. Mental clarity and motivation can be affected by alcohol.
  3. CBD is non-psychoactive and can aid in relaxation and sleep.
  4. THC is psychoactive and can affect heart rate and anxiety levels.
  5. Cannabis may be a safer alternative to alcohol for some individuals.
  6. Research on cannabis is still evolving, especially regarding its effects on performance.
  7. CBD shows promise as an anti-inflammatory, but its effects are not as strong as NSAIDs.

---- Partners ----

item.title: Reflections on the First 'States Debate' [Wolfie's World]
item.itunes:title: Reflections on the First 'States Debate' [Wolfie's World]
item.description:
The States Debates sparked a unique conversation about Western States.

Brian reflects on his performance evaluating his strengths and weaknesses.

He goes further to say that the current landscape of ultrarunners may not lionize WSER like the previous. This is an opportunity for WSER to progress and remain the premiuer American Ultra.

Also, WSER toes the line on appealing to amateurs and elites. Eventually, it might have to choose one.

item.title: Wind + Fire at Oklahoma Ultra, French Ultra Kit Requirements, & Training for 100+ Milers
item.itunes:title: Wind + Fire at Oklahoma Ultra, French Ultra Kit Requirements, & Training for 100+ Milers
item.description:
1. There was a wind event in Stillwater, OK causing problems at MidSouth Gravel. The crazy wind led to fires requiring runners to be extracted from the course.
  2. EcoTrail Paris - I'm running a 50 miler this weekend that starts outside of Paris and ends on the Eiffel Tower. Pack requirements are different than any event I've run in America.
  3. Ryan Ramsby ran Cocodona and is preparing for Tahoe 200 in 2025. We don't hear much about what really goes into training for these things. He's breaking down everything from training, to logistics, nutrition, gear, and more.

item.title: Is Western States Vulnerable to a New Premier American Trail Race? [States Debates]
item.itunes:title: Is Western States Vulnerable to a New Premier American Trail Race? [States Debates]
item.description:
STATES DEBATES - is the epitome of fandom. We're diving into States three months before race day to build anticipation and get even more excited than years past.

Western States is the most enjoyable race to watch in all of American Ultrarunning. It's a national championship of sorts that enjoys resting at the top of all American events.

But can it maintain that dominance or is it vulnerable?

Brian takes the position that its vulnerable and Troy of the MidPacker Pod thinks its guaranteed to maintain its spot as the premier.

As time goes on, we'll debate more specifically about the 2025 field, but until then we're enjoying debate topics that transcend the 2025 event.

Enjoy.

item.title: Training, Recovery, Nutrition, and Hydration: Trail Runner Juggling [Wolfie's World]
item.itunes:title: Training, Recovery, Nutrition, and Hydration: Trail Runner Juggling [Wolfie's World]
item.description:
In this episode of Wolfies World, Brian discusses the concept of being a 'running juggler' in ultra running, emphasizing the importance of balancing various aspects of training, recovery, nutrition, and hydration.

He highlights the need for strength training, the impact of sleep on performance, and the significance of proper fueling during runs.

The conversation encourages listeners to be mindful of their training routines and to focus on areas that may need improvement to maximize their performance on race day.

item.title: How Many TRAIL RUNNERS Are There?
item.itunes:title: How Many TRAIL RUNNERS Are There?
item.description:
Ever wondered how many trail runners are out there? From casual joggers to ultra-marathon enthusiasts, trail running has become an increasingly popular sport.

But just how many people are lacing up their trail running shoes and hitting the trails? Mikey Yablong, who currently attends Dartmouth University, co-authored a study aiming to answer this question along with dozens more.

You'll be surprised by the numbers.

The Study.

item.title: How to Actually Finish an Ultra Marathon
item.itunes:title: How to Actually Finish an Ultra Marathon
item.description:
I chatted with Elsa Jaworski about how she keeps running when she wants to quit. She has ran multiple 200 mile+ races, multiple 100 mile races, and more.

In this episode we explore how she keeps going when she wants to quit.

It's the most important question for us novices who are endeavoring to do something as big as an ultramarathon.

This is a throwback to the very first episode of this podcast.

item.title: [Wolfie’s World] The Greatest Era of Running | Running as Performance Art
item.itunes:title: [Wolfie’s World] The Greatest Era of Running | Running as Performance Art
item.description:
We are in the greatest era for runners, blending performance and self-expression into 'performance art.'

He reflects on the cultural influences of running, comparing it to past eras of other cultural movements, and explores the potential for running to leave a lasting legacy in the broader cultural landscape.

Brian encourages listeners to embrace their unique expressions in running and to consider how the sport can continue to evolve and influence future generations.

item.title: [Wolfie’s World] Running Gear: Performance or Persona?
item.itunes:title: [Wolfie’s World] Running Gear: Performance or Persona?
item.description:
Today on Wolfie's World, Brian analyzes gear as persona versus gear as performance.

For years, gear was optimized for performance and brands were built around products. Now, brands are emerging that appeal to the personas we're all building out on the ultratrail.

Wolfie works through the 'running as self expression' list of brands and breaks down the movement.

item.title: Running Nutrition: What you ACTUALLY need
item.itunes:title: Running Nutrition: What you ACTUALLY need
item.description:
Josh and Jesse Rich discuss the legitimacy of various nutrition products. What are the products that are worth the money? How can we make some of this stuff at home and save the money? What ever happened to Spring Energy and its Awesomesauce debacle?

Follow Jesse for deeper insights and homemade running nutrition insights.

Takeaways

  1. High sodium intake is essential for some runners during long events.
  2. Budget-friendly nutrition options are available for runners.
  3. Real foods can be beneficial for slower runs.
  4. Runners can batch homemade nutrition for convenience.

item.title: Altra and Topo Athletic: Wildly Similar/Different Shoes
item.itunes:title: Altra and Topo Athletic: Wildly Similar/Different Shoes
item.description:
Josh Rosenthal and Taylor Bodin (Lead Trail Reviewer, Believe in the Run) discuss the evolution of Altra and Topo Athletic - two running shoe brands that have become staples at every ultramarathon starting line.

In recent years they have been competing for similar types of runners. And when brands compete, runners win.

I didn’t know much about Topo Athletic going into this discussion. I look forward to giving them a try.

Altra - Lone Peak 8 Review

Topo Athletic - Ultraventure 3 Review

item.title: Nutrition Myths for Ultra Runners
item.itunes:title: Nutrition Myths for Ultra Runners
item.description:
Zoe Rom and Kyle Van Horn host Your Diet Sucks, a podcast for runners about how to unf*ck your food.

In this episode we discuss the complexities of nutrition for ultra runners.

  1. Common misconceptions (is milk useful midrace fuel?)
  2. Importance of a structured fueling plan
  3. Psychological aspects of food choices in the context of athletic performance.

The conversation emphasizes the need for a nuanced understanding of diet, the role of community in dietary choices, and practical tips for optimizing nutrition during races.

item.title: Has Western States Peaked as the Premier American Ultramarathon? [Wolfie's World]
item.itunes:title: Has Western States Peaked as the Premier American Ultramarathon? [Wolfie's World]
item.description:
Today's episode combines Wolfie's World and Bad Runners Take into one episode.

  1. States Debates - Wolfie believes that Western States in vulnerable to being dethroned in the next 5 years as the premier American ultra. Who will debate him? Email me at hello@borderlands.cc if you're interested in joining us for a show.
  2. Wolfie's World, episode 10 - 90% of podcasts don't make it to episode 10. Let's celebrate by reliving Woflie's crew experience with Norda elite Elliot Cardin at Black Canyons.
  3. Golden Tickets winners didn't get prize money at Black Canyons. Should Borderlands pay them? Maybe.
  4. Our trail running league is taking shape and a lot of people want to help us make it happen.
